drills.name: Mikan Drill
drills.setup: Player starts under the basket on one side of the hoop. No defender.
steps: Shoot a right-handed layup using the backboard box.
Without letting the ball hit the floor, catch the rebound and immediately move to the left side.
Shoot a left-handed layup from the other side.
Repeat continuously for 60 seconds — the goal is rhythm and touch, not speed.
cues: Use the backboard box every time — not a swish. Teach the angle early.
Jump off the inside foot on each side: right foot for left layup, left foot for right.
Keep the ball high throughout — no dipping it to your waist between shots.
variations: Power Mikan: two-foot jump stop and power up instead of a running layup.
Reverse Mikan: shoot reverse layups from each side, no backboard.
Timed challenge: how many made layups in 60 seconds?

drills.name: Two-Ball Dribbling
drills.setup: Each player needs two basketballs. Start stationary in an athletic stance, feet shoulder-width apart.
steps: Dribble both balls simultaneously at the same height for 20 reps.
Alternate the balls — one goes down as the other comes up — for 20 reps.
Walk forward down the lane using the alternating dribble, eyes up.
Return with simultaneous dribble. Progress to full court when comfortable.
cues: Push the ball down — don't wait for it or slap it. You control it.
Head completely up after the first trip — if you're looking at the ball, it's too hard.
Stay in your stance. The minute you stand straight, you lose control.
variations: One high, one low: one ball at waist height, one ball at knee height.
Crossover series: cross both balls at the same time, then alternate.
Two-ball figure-8 through the legs for advanced players.

drills.name: 3-Man Weave
drills.setup: Three players line up across the baseline — one in the middle, one at each sideline. Middle player starts with the ball.
steps: Middle passes to either wing, then cuts diagonally behind that player.
Wing catches, passes to the far wing cutting toward them, then goes behind that player.
Continue weaving all the way down the court — pass and move behind.
The player who receives the ball at the far end finishes with a layup.
cues: Pass and GO — your cut starts the moment the ball leaves your hands.
Run to the outside of whoever you passed to — not behind the play.
Call the name of who you're passing to every single time.
variations: Add a full-court press defense after the finish.
Add a fourth player as a weak-side rebounder on the return.
Two-ball weave: two balls in play simultaneously for advanced groups.

drills.name: Defensive Slide Series
drills.setup: Players line up on the baseline in a low defensive stance — knees bent, back flat, hands active.
steps: Coach calls a direction. Players slide without crossing their feet for 4–5 steps.
Coach calls 'Sprint!' — players sprint forward to the next line, then reset into stance.
Coach calls 'Back!' — defensive drop step, turn and sprint, recover into stance.
Run the full sequence from baseline to half court and back.
cues: If you can only hold your stance for 3 seconds, you're not low enough. Get lower.
Push off the back foot — step with the lead foot first, drag the back foot. Never cross.
Head stays level — no bobbing. If your head moves, your hips are too high.
variations: Add a ball: player mirrors a coach dribbling down the floor.
Paired slides: two players face each other and mirror — whoever crosses feet does push-ups.
Add a close-out at the end: sprint to a cone, chop feet, contest an imaginary shot.

drills.name: Form Shooting
drills.setup: Player stands 3–5 feet directly in front of the basket. One ball. No defender. No pressure.
steps: Hold the ball on your shooting hand only — guide hand rests lightly on the side.
Start with the ball at forehead or chin level. Elbow under the ball, pointed at the rim.
Extend straight up and release at the top — follow through, fingers pointing down.
10 makes, then take one small step back.
cues: BEEF: Balance → Eyes on the rim → Elbow under the ball → Follow Through.
Hold your follow-through until the ball hits the rim or net. Every single rep.
Guide hand is a shelf — it holds the ball up but comes off before the release.
variations: One-hand shooting: remove the guide hand and shoot with one hand from 3 feet.
Seated shooting: sit in a chair and shoot overhead to build arm feel.
Eyes closed: 5 shots with eyes closed after your form is locked in.

drills.name: Cone Dribble Course
drills.setup: Set up 6 cones in a straight line, 3 feet apart. Players line up at the baseline.
steps: Dribble through the cones with the right hand only, weaving between each cone.
At the last cone, execute a crossover and return with the left hand.
Third pass: add a move at each cone — crossover, between the legs, or behind the back.
Fourth pass: attack the cones at game speed.
cues: Get low — bend your knees, not your back.
Change speeds: in control through cones, explode out of the last one.
Eyes up after the first two trips — no looking at the ball.
variations: Timed: how fast can each player complete the course without losing the ball?
Add a finish: last cone leads into a layup at the basket.
Zigzag course instead of straight line — forces more acute change-of-direction.

drills.name: Elbow Jumpers
drills.setup: Player starts at one elbow. Rebounder or rack at the basket.
steps: Receive a pass at the elbow in a ready-to-shoot stance — feet already set.
Catch and shoot, OR shot fake + one dribble pull-up. Alternate between the two.
Rebounder outlets back to the passer. Player sprints to the opposite elbow.
Repeat for 5 minutes and track makes per elbow.
cues: Catch ready to shoot — don't catch flat-footed and then set your feet.
Shot fake must be convincing — ball comes up to your face, knees flex, head moves.
Follow through and hold it every single time.
variations: Dribble-only version: player catches, takes two hard dribbles, pull-up from the elbow.
Add a defender with one hand in the passing lane.
5-makes-and-move: hit 5 makes at each elbow before progressing outward.

drills.name: Shell Drill
drills.setup: 4 offensive players in a 4-out set. 4 defenders guard them. Ball starts at one wing. Run multiple groups simultaneously if you have more than 8 players.
steps: Offense passes the ball around the perimeter — no dribbles, no cuts to start.
On every pass, defenders rotate: on-ball pressures, others adjust to ball-side and help-side.
Coach freezes play and checks defender positions. Correct before resuming.
Progress over sessions: add one dribble, then live cuts, then full live defense.
cues: On-ball: get in the passing lane — no direct line to the basket.
One pass away: see both your player AND the ball at all times.
Two passes away: drop into the paint. You should see the entire offense.
variations: Add a post player to force help-side decisions.
Live scoring: offense tries to score, defense gets a point for each stop.
Run shell drill in the half-lane to force tighter rotations.

drills.name: Pressure Passing
drills.setup: Pairs of players 10–12 feet apart. One is the passer, one is a soft defender — no stealing, just pressure.
steps: Passer holds ball in triple threat. Defender takes up space — hands active, no reaching.
Passer must throw a crisp chest pass, bounce pass, or overhead pass through the pressure.
Rotate roles every 60 seconds. Run 3 rounds per pair.
Progress: defender can now tip but not steal. Then add live deflections.
cues: Step into every pass — the step generates half the power.
Protect the ball before you throw it — pivot, use your body, see the window.
Aim for the target's hands, not their chest.
variations: Double the defender: two defenders, one passer.
Add a second receiver: passer picks which window to hit.
Timed: how many clean catches in 30 seconds?

drills.name: Pivot and Pass
drills.setup: Partners 8–10 feet apart. Ball starts with one player. Mark a small X on the floor as the pivot spot.
steps: Catch the pass, land, and immediately establish a pivot foot — announce which one.
Execute a front pivot (step forward to face your partner) and make a crisp pass.
Next catch: use a rear pivot (step back to face your partner) instead.
Alternate front and rear pivot on every catch for 2 minutes, then switch roles.
cues: Pick your pivot foot and DON'T move it. That's a travel.
Stay on the ball of your pivot foot — the heel coming down is a travel too.
Protect the ball during the pivot: elbows out, ball close to your body.
variations: Add a chair as a defender — pivot away from it.
Triple threat progression: catch, pivot into triple threat, shot fake before passing.
Jab step + pivot: establish the jab, then rear pivot to create a passing angle.

drills.name: 1v1 Closeout
drills.setup: Offensive player at the 3-point line. Defender starts at the opposite elbow — about 15 feet away.
steps: Defender passes to the offensive player and immediately sprints to close out.
Sprint the first 3/4 of the distance, then take short choppy steps and raise hands.
Offense attacks live — one dribble maximum before a shot or drive decision.
Defender contains and contests. Rotate after each rep.
cues: Short choppy steps at the end — you can't slide if you're still running full speed.
High hands on the close-out: take away the shot first, then force a drive.
Pick a side to force and commit — baseline OR middle.
variations: Expand to 2v2 with a help-side defender.
Shooter's bonus: offense scores 2 points for made shots off a bad closeout.
Add a shot fake — defender must not leave their feet.

drills.name: Crossover Series
drills.setup: Players spread out at half court with one ball each.
steps: In-place crossover: 30 reps, eyes up, stay low.
Walking crossover: advance down the court with continuous crossovers.
Speed series: sprint + crossover at half court, sprint back.
Full combination: crossover → between the legs → behind the back → hesitation step.
cues: The crossover is low and fast — the ball should stay below your knee.
Your body sells the move. Shoulder dip and head fake before your hand goes.
Push through the dribble, don't pull it across.
variations: Crossover into an explosion step — change of pace, not just direction.
Two-ball crossover: both balls cross at the same time.
Timed gauntlet: 10 cones, crossover at each one.

drills.name: Star Passing
drills.setup: 5 players form a large star spread across the lane. One ball to start.
steps: Player 1 passes to Player 3 (skip one person in the star pattern) and follows their pass.
Player 3 passes to Player 5 and follows. The pattern continues.
Keep the ball moving in the skip pattern for 2 minutes.
Reverse the direction. Add a second ball for an advanced group.
cues: Pass to a target — hands out, eyes on the passer. Call for the ball.
Follow your pass immediately. The rotation breaks down when someone hesitates.
Every catch goes into triple threat.
variations: Two balls simultaneously.
Alternate pass types: chest on odd passes, bounce on even.
Timed: how many clean rotations in 60 seconds?

drills.name: Post Footwork Series
drills.setup: Player on the low block. Passer at the wing. Optional passive defender behind the post player.
steps: Player seals and receives on the block — catch with two hands, immediately into a protective stance.
Drop step baseline: one big step baseline, power dribble, two-foot finish.
Drop step middle: one big step middle, power dribble, finish.
Up-and-under: catch, shot fake, one step, finish on the other side.
cues: Seal first, THEN call for the ball.
Catch and immediately protect — elbows out, ball tight.
Every move starts with a shot fake.
variations: Add a live defender who contests but doesn't steal.
Full 1v1 live: offense tries to score.
Add a kick-out option: drive, get doubled, kick to a shooter at the elbow.

drills.name: Skip Pass Series
drills.setup: 5 offensive players in a 5-out set. No defense to start.
steps: Ball swings to one wing. Players move and cut to create skip pass opportunities on the weak side.
Wing throws a skip pass across the key — the receiver is already in a shot-ready stance.
Receiver catches, shot fakes, and skip passes back.
After 3 consecutive skip passes, the fourth catch is live — attack or shoot.
cues: Skip passes go over the defense — throw it high if the lane is clogged.
Receiver: be ready to shoot while the ball is in the air. Not after you catch it.
Weak-side players: cut and move. Standing still makes the skip pass harder.
variations: Add a weak-side closeout defender.
Live 5v5 with a skip pass requirement per possession.
Countdown scoring: each skip + shot = 2 points, normal baskets = 1.

drills.name: Full-Court Layup Lines
drills.setup: Two lines at half court — one ball line, one rebounder line. Ball line is on the right side.
steps: First player in ball line dribbles full speed to the right side and finishes a right-hand layup.
First player in rebounder line sprints to retrieve the make or miss before it hits the floor.
Rebounder outlets a pass to the next person in ball line and rotates to the end of that line.
Shooter rotates to the end of the rebounder line. Run continuously for 3 minutes, then switch sides.
cues: Two dribbles max from half court — attack the basket, not the midrange.
Eyes on the box before you release — don't look at the ball on the way up.
Rebounder: your job starts before the ball goes up. Sprint before the shot.
variations: Opposite hand only: force all finishes with the left hand.
Add a token defender who takes a charge position — shooters must read and adjust.
Timed scoring: how many makes in 3 minutes?

drills.name: Dribble Tag
drills.setup: All players dribble a ball in a contained half-court area. Designate 1–2 taggers who also dribble.
steps: All players must dribble their ball continuously — lose the dribble, do 5 push-ups and rejoin.
Taggers try to knock away another player's ball while protecting their own.
A player whose ball is knocked out is frozen — they can only resume after a teammate gives them a five.
Rotate taggers every 90 seconds. Shrink the court area as the group improves.
cues: Keep your body between the tagger and the ball. Use your body, not just your hand.
Eyes up — you can't see the tagger if you're watching the ball.
Change speeds and change direction. Straight lines get you tagged.
variations: Two balls: each player dribbles two balls at once. Nearly impossible and extremely fun.
Off hand only: dribble with the weak hand the entire time.
Predator: the tagged player becomes an additional tagger. Last player standing wins.

drills.name: 3-on-2 Continuous
drills.setup: 3 offensive players at half court, 2 defenders in the paint. Extra players wait at half court.
steps: 3 attackers push into a 3-on-2 fast break. Defenders work to stop or force a tough shot.
After a score or defensive stop, the 2 defenders immediately become 2 of 3 attackers going the other way.
One of the original 3 attackers stays back as a defender. The other two wait at half court.
The new group of 3 grabs a player from the waiting line and attacks the opposite end.
cues: Offense: the middle player controls the break — pass only when the defense commits to you.
Defense: one guards the ball, one protects the rim. Don't both go to the ball.
Transition is instant — if you stop to think, the break is gone.
variations: Add a trailer: a 4th offensive player who fills the lane 2 steps behind the break.
Limit to 1 dribble per possession — forces quicker passing decisions.
Scoring: fast break baskets count 2, half-court baskets count 1.

drills.name: Around the World
drills.setup: Mark 7 spots around the arc: corner, wing, elbow, top, opposite elbow, opposite wing, opposite corner.
steps: Player starts at the first corner and shoots. Make it — advance to the next spot. Miss — stay or use a chance shot.
A chance shot is a second attempt from the same spot. Make it — advance. Miss the chance — go back to start.
Work all the way around the arc and back. First player to complete the full circuit wins.
Solo version: race against a clock. How fast can you complete the circuit making every shot?
cues: Don't rush the make-or-miss decision. Take a breath and shoot your shot.
Square your feet to each new spot — don't carry your body angle from the last position.
Swish or bank — just make it. Both count.
variations: Bank shot rule: every shot must use the backboard — great for teaching bank angles.
Off hand: complete the circuit shooting with the weak hand only.
Add mid-range spots inside the arc for younger players who can't reach the 3-point line.

drills.name: Machine Gun Passing
drills.setup: One passer stands in the middle of the lane. All other players form a single line facing them, each with a ball.
steps: First player in line passes their ball to the middle player, who catches and immediately passes it back.
The first player catches the return pass and steps to the end of the line.
Second player fires their pass before the first player is even out of the way — continuous rhythm.
Run for 60 seconds, count clean exchanges, rest 20 seconds, repeat. Rotate the middle player.
cues: Middle player: hands up and ready before the ball leaves the passer's hands.
Line players: fire the pass the instant you're in position — this is a rhythm drill, not a polite one.
Chest pass or bounce pass only — no fancy stuff. Speed and accuracy.
variations: Weak hand only: all passes must be made and caught with the non-dominant hand.
Two middles: two players in the middle create two simultaneous passing lanes.
Add a defender in the lane who tries to tip passes — now the passer must read the window.

drills.name: 5-Spot Shooting
drills.setup: Mark 5 spots: two corners, two wings, and the top of the key. Passer at the elbow or rebounder at the basket.
steps: Player starts at spot 1 and catches a pass in a ready-to-shoot stance. Shoot immediately.
Sprint to spot 2 before the rebounder has even passed the ball — be in your stance by the time it arrives.
Work through all 5 spots. One full circuit = one round.
Track makes out of 5 each round. Goal: hit at least 3 of 5 consistently before moving the spots back.
cues: Sprint between spots — if you're jogging, you're not simulating game speed.
Don't adjust your feet after the catch. Arrive ready. If you're not ready, you left the last spot too slow.
Same shot every time. Rhythm, not power.
variations: Shot fake + one dribble pull-up from each spot instead of a catch-and-shoot.
Pressure circuit: player must hit 3 in a row or resets to spot 1.
Move all 5 spots to mid-range for younger or developing shooters.

drills.name: Defensive Zig-Zag
drills.setup: Pairs of players. Offensive player starts at the corner with the ball. Defender is in a low stance in front of them.
steps: Offense dribbles diagonally toward the sideline. Defender slides to cut off the angle.
When the defender successfully cuts off the path, offense changes direction — defender pivots and slides the other way.
Continue zig-zagging from baseline to half court.
No stealing, no blocks — defense is working footwork and positioning only.
cues: Force the change of direction with your body, not your hands — no reaching.
Beat the dribbler to the spot. If you're reacting, you're already late.
Stay square — every change of direction, reset your hips. Don't let them get sideways.
variations: Live zig-zag: offense can now try to blow by the defender for real.
Increase dribbler speed each trip — defender must adjust to faster offensive players.
Add a finish: when they reach half court, the drill goes live to the basket.

drills.name: Box-Out Battle
drills.setup: Pairs of players inside the paint. Coach or passer has the ball at the free-throw line.
steps: Passer holds the ball and both players position themselves — one on offense, one on defense.
Passer says 'Shot!' and simulates a shot (or shoots a real one if there's a basket available).
Defender must make contact, pivot into the offensive player, and maintain their box-out for a full 3 seconds.
Whoever emerges with the rebound wins the rep. Switch roles after each rep.
cues: Contact first, then pivot — if you pivot before you touch them, you have nothing to push against.
Wide base, low center of gravity. If you're standing straight, they'll go right around you.
Keep moving your feet. A box-out isn't a statue — it's active work.
variations: 2-on-2 box-out: two rebounders vs two offensive players — introduces traffic and screening.
Tip drill: instead of catching the rebound, tip it to a teammate three times before securing.
Outlet pass: after securing the rebound, immediately make a crisp outlet to a waiting guard.

drills.name: Speed Dribble Relay
drills.setup: Two teams in lines at the baseline. One ball per team. A cone or marker at half court.
steps: First player dribbles at full speed to the half-court cone using the right hand.
At the cone, execute a crossover and speed dribble back with the left hand.
Tag the next player's hand — they go immediately. Dropped ball = player starts over from where it was dropped.
First team to have every player complete the trip wins.
cues: Push the ball out in front when you're at full speed — don't slap it at your side.
The crossover at half court is low and fast. This is not the time to be pretty about it.
Head up the whole time — even in a relay, build the habit.
variations: Off hand only: entire relay completed with the weak hand — no crossover allowed.
Add a figure-8 around two cones at half court before returning.
Individual time trial: no teams, each player races the clock instead.

drills.name: 4-on-4 Half Court
drills.setup: 4 offensive players vs 4 defenders in a half-court set. Extra players wait at half court.
steps: Offense runs their half-court set — 4-out, motion, or whatever the team is working on.
Defense applies half-court pressure. Call out switches or rotations out loud.
Score = offense wins the rep and stays on. Stop = defense wins and transitions to offense.
Next group in from the sideline plays defense against the winners.
cues: Offense: every action should have a read. Cut if your defender turns their head.
Defense: communicate before you help, not while you're already scrambling.
Out loud, every time — offense calls screens, defense calls 'ball' and 'help'.
variations: Add a shot clock (15 seconds) — forces quicker decision-making.
Limit dribbles per player per possession to 3 — forces movement and passing.
Winners-stay format with a 3-win cap — everyone cycles through.

drills.name: Reaction Closeout
drills.setup: Defender starts under the basket. Shooter is at the 3-point line. Coach stands at the elbow with a ball.
steps: Defender faces away from the shooter — eyes on the coach.
Coach makes a random pass signal (verbal or physical). Defender turns and sprints to close out.
Shooter catches and immediately attacks: shoot, drive, or shot fake and drive.
Defender must contest, contain, and prevent an easy basket. Rotate after each rep.
cues: The reaction test is the whole point — no peeking. The drill only works if the defender is truly surprised.
High hands and choppy steps on arrival. You're late by definition — don't compound it by running through them.
Pick a side and force it before they decide for you.
variations: Multiple shooters: coach randomizes which shooter receives the pass — tests peripheral vision.
Add a pass fake: coach fakes a pass to test if the defender bites.
2v2 reaction: two defenders face away, two shooters attack simultaneously.

drills.name: Beat the Pro
drills.setup: Player picks 5 spots around the arc or mid-range. Imaginary opponent starts at 0 points.
steps: Player shoots from spot 1. Make it — player scores 2. Miss — the 'pro' scores 2.
Work through all 5 spots in order. Keep running score after each shot.
If the pro reaches 10 first, player loses. If player reaches 10 first, player wins.
Track wins and losses over a full week. Challenge: go 5-0 in a session.
cues: This is a pressure drill — treat every shot like it matters, because in this drill it does.
Routine: same footwork, same breath, same follow-through on every single shot.
When you're losing, slow down. Rushing the next shot is how the pro wins.
variations: Raise the stakes: pro needs 7 to win, player still needs 10. Creates real pressure.
Two-ball: passer fires balls in quick succession — no rest between shots.
Full-game version: play to 21, alternating shot types (catch-and-shoot, off the dribble).

drills.name: Jab Step Series
drills.setup: Player catches a pass at the wing and establishes a pivot foot immediately. Passive defender optional.
steps: Catch and land in triple threat — ball at hip, knees bent, weight on the balls of your feet.
Execute a jab step: short, sharp step directly at the defender's lead foot. Return to triple threat.
Jab and shoot: if the defender freezes or backs up on the jab, shoot immediately.
Jab and go: if the defender doesn't respect the jab, attack the space they gave you.
cues: The jab is a question — you're asking 'what are you going to do?' and reacting to the answer.
Keep the ball in triple threat the whole time. The second the ball drops below your hip, you've lost the read.
Short and sharp — a long jab step is a travel waiting to happen.
variations: Add a live defender: they respond authentically to the jab — shooter must read and react.
Two-dribble pull-up after the jab-and-go — attack the gap and pull up at the elbow.
Jab, rip, go: jab step, rip the ball through low to protect it, attack off the rip.

drills.name: Full-Court 3-on-3
drills.setup: 3v3 full court. Extra players wait at half court. Score to 5 — winners stay, losers sub out.
steps: Game starts with a check ball at half court. Full court, full rules.
Call your own fouls — enforces accountability and teaches clean play.
When a team scores, the opposing team checks it at half court from out of bounds — no cherry picking.
Losing team sprints a full-court slide before the next group comes in.
cues: 3-on-3 exposes you. There's nowhere to hide — use every rep.
Set a screen or cut on every possession. Isolation ball loses 3-on-3.
Talk on defense. With only 3 defenders, communication isn't optional.
variations: One-dribble limit: forces constant motion and quick decision-making.
Make-it-take-it: scoring team keeps possession — rewards efficiency.
Alternate ball possession after each score — equalizes possessions and makes defense matter more.

drills.name: Transition Defense Sprint
drills.setup: All players on the baseline. Cones at the FT line, half court, opposite FT line, and opposite baseline.
steps: On the whistle, all players sprint to the near free-throw line and back to baseline.
Sprint to half court and back. Sprint to the far free-throw line and back. Sprint full court and back.
30-second rest after each full sequence. Run 4 sequences total.
Add a defensive slide from baseline to FT line on the last sequence — finish with footwork.
cues: Touch every line. If your foot doesn't touch the line, the sprint doesn't count.
Arms drive the legs — pump them hard on the back half when you're tired.
This is a trust drill: the team runs together, finishes together.
variations: Competitive: last player to touch each line does 5 push-ups.
Ball in hand: each player dribbles during the sprint — adds coordination under fatigue.
Defensive shuffle on the return legs — sprint there, slide back.

drills.name: Ball Fake Series
drills.setup: Player in triple threat at the wing. Passive or active defender in front of them.
steps: Pass fake: look off a defender with your eyes and head before making the real pass.
Shot fake: lift the ball to your face, flex your knees, and watch the defender leave their feet.
Drive fake: jab one direction, freeze the defender, attack the opposite gap.
Chain fakes: pass fake → shot fake → attack. The sequence creates progressively more confusion.
cues: Your eyes sell the fake before your hands do anything. Look where you're NOT going.
A shot fake only works if it looks exactly like your real shot. Use your legs.
After the fake, attack immediately — hesitating after a fake gives the defense time to recover.
variations: Live defense: defender tries to time the real move. Forces the player to commit to the fake.
Consecutive fakes: player can't attack until they've thrown at least 2 fakes.
Film it: record one rep and watch the defender's reaction. Extremely effective teaching tool.

drills.name: Weak Hand Wall Dribble
drills.setup: Each player stands 2 feet from a flat wall with one basketball. Off-hand only.
steps: Dribble the ball against the wall with the weak hand — aim at a spot on the wall at about knee height.
Start slow and controlled for 30 seconds. Focus on finger control, not palm dribbling.
Increase to medium pace for 30 seconds — ball should be bouncing off the wall at a consistent angle.
Full speed for 30 seconds. Rest and repeat 3 rounds total.
cues: Fingertips only. If your palm is hitting the ball, you're slapping — that's not dribbling.
Consistent angle = consistent return. Change the angle, and you'll chase the ball.
The wall doesn't lie — if your dribble is sloppy, the return will be sloppy.
variations: Low dribble: aim below knee height on the wall — forces a tighter, more controlled touch.
Alternating hands: 10 reps left, 10 reps right, 10 reps left. No stopping between transitions.
Eyes closed: once the rhythm is locked in, close your eyes and dribble by feel for 15 seconds.

drills.name: Outlet Pass Drill
drills.setup: Rebounder at the low block. Outlet receiver at the sideline hash mark. Coach or passer at the free-throw line.
steps: Coach simulates a missed shot. Rebounder secures it with two hands and chins the ball.
Rebounder pivots to the outlet side, locates the receiver, and makes a sharp overhead or baseball pass.
Outlet receiver catches, advances the ball to half court, and hits a trailer cutting into the lane.
Trailer finishes with a layup. All three players rotate positions after each rep.
cues: Chin the ball after the rebound — protect it before you pivot. Don't start your outlet before you have it.
Outlet to the hash mark, not the corner. The receiver needs space to advance.
Outlet receiver: catch facing the basket. Your first look is always the length of the floor.
variations: Contested rebound: add a weak-side offensive rebounder who fights for the ball.
Push it full court: outlet → push pass → layup, no more than 4 passes total.
Live 3-on-2: after the outlet, the rebounder joins the break — 3 attackers vs 2 waiting defenders.

drills.name: Contested Mid-Range
drills.setup: Shooter at the elbow or mid-range wing. Defender closes out with one hand up — no blocking allowed.
steps: Passer fires a quick pass. Defender closes out with a high hand in the shooting lane.
Shooter must read the contest: if hand is in the lane, shot fake and drive. If not, shoot over it.
Shooter gets 2 points for a made shot over a real contest, 1 point for made shot after a fake.
Run 5 minutes per player. Track total points.
cues: See the hand before you decide. Don't preset your read before the catch.
Shooting over a contest is a skill — get your release point higher, not faster.
The shot fake must fully sell. If the defender doesn't move, you didn't fake — you paused.
variations: Full live 1v1: no restrictions on the defender after the catch.
Three-shot rule: make 3 contested shots in a row before rotating to a new spot.
Off the dribble: player receives the pass, takes one dribble to create separation, then shoots.

drills.name: Deny the Ball
drills.setup: Ball handler at the top of the key. Wing player and defender one pass away. Second passer at the opposite wing.
steps: Defender gets in denial stance: one hand in the passing lane, see both player and ball.
Offensive wing tries to get open by V-cutting, backdoor cutting, or changing speeds.
If wing is denied for 5 seconds, they go backdoor and the top player lobs or bounces it in.
If the pass is completed, it goes live — defender must recover and contain.
cues: One hand in the lane, one eye on the ball. If you're watching only the player, you'll get back-cut.
Your denial position moves with the offensive player — stay between them and the ball.
When they go backdoor, switch to ball-side and get behind them. Don't chase across the lane.
variations: Three-player rotation: add a cutter from the weak side — defender must choose.
Live possession after entry: once the ball is caught, play live 2-on-2.
Post denial: move the drill to the low block — teach fronting the post rather than wing denial.

drills.name: Kill the Grass
drills.setup: Each player has a ball. Spread out in the half court, at least 3 feet between players.
steps: Dribble the ball as hard as possible straight down — imagine pounding it through the floor.
Stay in your athletic stance: knees bent, back flat, head up.
Coach calls out a hand: 'Right!' or 'Left!' — switch immediately without looking at the ball.
Run 4 rounds of 30 seconds each with 15 seconds rest between rounds.
cues: Hard dribble = better control. A soft dribble is harder to control, not easier.
Fingertips, not your palm. You're pushing the ball down, not slapping it.
Head up every round. The first round it's okay to peek — after that, no more looking.
variations: Two-ball Kill the Grass: both hands pounding simultaneously.
Move while dribbling: walk around the half court without looking at the ball.
Coach calls 'freeze' — every player stops dribbling and holds their stance. Check posture.

drills.name: Red Light Green Light
drills.setup: All players line up on the baseline with a ball each. Coach stands at the opposite baseline.
steps: Coach calls 'Green light!' — players dribble toward the coach as fast as they can.
Coach calls 'Red light!' — players must stop immediately with a jump stop: two feet land at the same time, no sliding.
Any player who takes an extra step or slides on the stop goes back 5 steps.
First player to reach the coach (without getting caught moving on red) wins the round.
cues: Jump stop: both feet hit the floor at the same time. One foot first is a travel.
Balance on the stop — don't fall forward. Land with your weight on the balls of your feet.
Eyes on the coach the whole time. If you're watching your ball, you'll miss the signal.
variations: No ball: run the drill without a basketball first to teach the jump stop in isolation.
Add 'Yellow light': slow dribble only — tests speed control between full speed and a stop.
Triple threat on red light: players must land in triple threat position when stopped.

drills.name: Jump Stop Drill
drills.setup: All players on the baseline with a ball. Lines of no more than 10 — use both ends of the court if needed.
steps: Players dribble forward at a controlled pace toward the free-throw line.
At the free-throw line, pick up the dribble and execute a jump stop — two feet land simultaneously.
Hold the position for 2 seconds: check knees bent, back flat, ball protected at hip.
Pivot to face the basket, return dribbling to the baseline. Repeat 5 times each player.
cues: Small hop on the stop — the bigger the jump, the more off-balance you'll be on landing.
Both feet land at the same time. Practice this without the ball first if it's not clicking.
After the stop, you have two pivot feet — use that. Too many players forget they have options.
variations: Increase the dribble speed: jog, then 75%, then full speed into the stop.
Add a pass: jump stop, pivot to find a partner, make a crisp pass before pivoting back.
Jump stop + shot fake: land, fake, drive. Chains the stop into a live offensive skill.

drills.name: Wall Passing
drills.setup: Each player stands 8–10 feet from a flat wall with one basketball. No partner needed.
steps: Chest pass: throw a crisp two-handed chest pass into the wall, catch the return, repeat for 30 seconds.
Bounce pass: same distance — throw into the floor so the ball hits the wall at knee height and returns.
Overhead pass: both hands overhead, snap the wrists and throw into the wall from overhead.
Run 3 rounds of each pass type. Add pass fakes before each throw on the final round.
cues: Step into every pass — the step is where your power comes from, not your arms.
Wrist snap on the release: thumbs point down after the ball leaves your hands.
The wall doesn't lie — if the return bounce is wild, your pass was off. Self-correct.
variations: Dominant hand only: one-handed passes into the wall to build arm strength and control.
Move closer to the wall for quicker reaction reps — great for hand speed.
Add pass fakes: fake high, throw low. Fake left, throw right. The wall is the receiver.

drills.name: Monkey in the Middle
drills.setup: Groups of 3. Two passers stand 12–15 feet apart. One defender (the monkey) stands in the middle.
steps: The two outside players pass back and forth. The defender tries to tip or steal every pass.
Passers must use fakes, pivots, and different pass types to get the ball past the defender.
If the defender tips or steals the ball, they switch with the passer who threw it.
Run for 3 minutes, then rotate so every player has multiple turns in the middle.
cues: Don't telegraph: look one way, pass the other. The defender reads your eyes first.
Pass fakes make the defender move — one hard fake can open the whole passing lane.
Step around the defender if you need to — you can pivot and find a better angle.
variations: Tighten the space: move passers closer together — harder to throw past the defender.
Two defenders: now both outside players must really work to connect.
No bounce passes: chest and overhead only — forces more deliberate fakes.

drills.name: Chase Down Layups
drills.setup: Two lines at half court on the same side — one offensive line with balls, one defensive line right behind them.
steps: On the whistle, the first offensive player dribbles at full speed toward the basket.
The defender starts one step behind — they sprint to catch up and contest the layup.
Offensive player must finish through the contest — no slowing down, no stopping.
Rotate: offensive player goes to the defensive line, defender goes to the end of the offensive line.
cues: Offense: attack the basket like the defender isn't there. Slowing down makes it easier for them, not harder.
Use the backboard — a bank layup is more reliable under pressure than going for the swish.
Defense: don't foul, but be there. A hand near the ball changes the finish even without contact.
variations: Left hand only: all finishes with the non-dominant hand.
Reverse layup: offense must use the backboard from the opposite angle.
Add a passer: offense receives a pass at the free-throw line before the defender starts — tests catch-and-attack.

drills.name: 2-1-0 Dribbling
drills.setup: Players spread out on the baseline with one ball each. Three cones at the lane line extended, halfway, and 3/4 court.
steps: Phase 2 — two dribbles per move: at each cone, execute a move (crossover, between legs, behind back) using two dribbles.
Phase 1 — one dribble per move: same course, same moves, but only one dribble at each cone.
Phase 0 — no dribble between cones: execute the move, catch the ball, and immediately attack the next cone.
Run each phase twice before moving to the next. This is a progression — don't rush to phase 0.
cues: The goal is to make the move in fewer dribbles — tighter and more efficient each phase.
Stay low through every move. Standing straight between cones costs you 2 steps.
Eyes up from the start — the whole point is ball control without looking at the ball.
variations: Choose your move at each cone — don't predetermine. Reacts to a coach's call.
Add a live defender at the last cone — player attacks 1v1 off the final move.
Time it: same course, same moves — beat your previous time at phase 1.

drills.name: Score for Stops
drills.setup: 2-on-2 in the half court. Scoreboard on the wall or tracked verbally. Defense scores points, not offense.
steps: Offense attacks — their only job is to score. Defenders play live defense.
Defense earns 1 point for a stop (rebound, turnover, or forced miss).
Offense earns 0 points — they are the challenge, not the scorers.
Play to 5 defensive stops, then rotate the defensive team out. Track which defense reaches 5 fastest.
cues: Defense: you're playing for points now. Every stop matters — this isn't a punishment.
Communicate before the play, not during it. 'Ball!' 'Help!' 'Shot!' — call it early.
Offense: push hard. The defense only gets better if the offense makes them work.
variations: 3-on-3: more decisions, more communication required.
Bonus points: a charge drawn = 2 points. A block without a foul = 2 points.
Live from a closeout: defense starts under the basket, passes to offense, and closes out — starts game-like.

drills.name: 5v0 Pass and Cut
drills.setup: 5 offensive players in a 5-out set (one at each elbow, two wings, one top). No defense.
steps: Ball starts at the top. Player passes to either wing and immediately cuts hard to the basket.
If the cutter is open (within 2 steps of the basket), the wing feeds them for a layup.
If not open, the cutter clears to the weak-side corner and the next player fills the empty spot.
Run continuously for 5 minutes — every pass triggers a cut. No standing still.
cues: Cut hard or don't cut at all. A lazy cut doesn't get you open — it just clutters the lane.
Passer: eyes on the cutter the whole way. If they're open, hit them. If not, keep moving the ball.
Fill the empty spot immediately — the offense breaks down when someone doesn't replace.
variations: Add a rule: the ball must be passed to the cutter at least twice per minute — forces the passer to look.
Add 1 defender: now the cuts have real stakes. Who gets open?
Screen variation: instead of cutting, the passer sets a screen for a teammate on the wing.

drills.name: No Dribble Offense
drills.setup: 5-on-5 or 4-on-4 in the half court. Zero dribbles allowed for the entire possession.
steps: Offense must pass and cut to score — absolutely no dribbling. Catch and pass.
Any dribble = turnover. Ball goes to the defense immediately.
Defense plays live — they know no one can dribble, so they can jump passing lanes.
Play for 5 minutes, then allow 1 dribble per player per possession and compare the difference.
cues: Move before you catch, not after. You can't dribble to create space — you have to create it with your feet.
Every catch goes into triple threat. You have one second before you need to pass or cut.
Cutters: you have to get open — the passer can't bail you out with a dribble drive.
variations: 1-dribble limit: each player gets exactly one dribble per possession — forces deliberate use.
Full court no dribble: play ends at half court, ball goes back. Forces press-break thinking.
Scoring: each basket in no-dribble mode = 2 points. Each made basket with 1 dribble = 1 point.

drills.name: 3v3 Live from Closeout
drills.setup: 3 offensive players spaced at the 3-point line. 3 defenders under the basket. Coach or player has the ball at the top.
steps: Coach passes to any offensive player. All 3 defenders must close out simultaneously to their matchup.
On the catch, it's live — offense attacks, defense contains.
Offense has 4 dribbles max per possession to create and score.
After the possession ends, the next group of 3 rotates in on defense. Run continuously.
cues: Close out under control — choppy steps, high hand, weight back. Don't sprint through the shooter.
Communicate the pass: 'Ball!' the moment it leaves the coach's hands. Everyone closes out together.
Contain first, steal second. An overaggressive closeout gives up a drive every time.
variations: Random assignment: coach calls a player's name as they throw the pass — that player's defender has to sprint farther.
Offense gets 2 points for a shot made off a bad closeout — creates accountability.
Expand to 4v4 or 5v5 from a closeout for a more complete team defense rep.

drills.name: Triple Threat Progression
drills.setup: Players spread out on the baseline with one ball each. Passive defender optional for later stages.
steps: Catch or pick up the ball and immediately land in triple threat: ball at hip, knees bent, one foot slightly forward.
From triple threat: show shot fake — ball up to your face, head moves, knees flex. Return to triple threat.
From triple threat: jab step — short sharp step at an imaginary defender's foot. Return to triple threat.
Chain it: catch → triple threat → shot fake → jab → attack (drive or shoot). Run 10 reps each side.
cues: Triple threat means all three options are live: pass, shoot, or drive. If one is impossible, you're not in triple threat.
Ball stays at your hip — not held out in front, not pressed against your body.
The shot fake works because it looks exactly like your real shot. Use your knees, not just your arms.
variations: Add a live defender who guesses which threat is coming — offense must read and react.
Partner version: one player in triple threat, one calls 'shoot', 'drive', or 'pass' — player reacts.
Time it: how many full triple threat sequences (catch, fake, jab, attack) in 60 seconds?
